mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth indicates robust consumer demand, which typically leads to higher economic activity and inflation expectations, pushing the 10-year U.S. Treasury yield upwards over the next 20 trading days as investors anticipate tighter monetary policy or higher real rates.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward yield direction is negative in more than 55% of periods where RSAFS growth is positive, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in the unemployment rate signals a tightening labor market, increasing wage pressures and inflation expectations. This prompts bond investors to demand higher yields, driving the 10-year U.S. Treasury yield upwards over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls by more than 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.5% in t-1mo, declining from peak
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward yield direction is negative in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE declines by the specified trigger, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains low despite an extremely low federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS), it indicates that monetary policy is highly accommodative but not yet generating significant inflationary pressures. This environment supports lower long-term yields as real rates remain suppressed, driving the 10-year U.S. Treasury yield downwards over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 235.982 (low YoY growth), FEDFUNDS at 0.12
failure_condition: If the 60-day forward yield direction is positive in more than 60% of periods where both CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS meet the specified conditions, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A strong rebound in industrial production signals a robust recovery in the supply side of the economy, which can initially be disinflationary as supply catches up with demand. This temporary disinflationary pressure could lead to a slight downward pressure on long-term yields over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 5-day forward yield direction is positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in housing starts reflects strong underlying demand and investment in the economy, which typically translates into broader economic expansion and higher inflation expectations. This upward pressure on economic activity drives the 10-year U.S. Treasury yield upwards over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 60-day forward yield direction is negative in more than 50% of periods where HOUST shows positive year-over-year growth, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in non-farm payrolls indicates robust job creation and a strengthening labor market, which typically leads to higher consumer spending and inflation. This positive economic outlook pushes the 10-year U.S. Treasury yield upwards over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases by more than 200,000 persons month-over-month
failure_condition: If the 5-day forward yield direction is negative in more than 50% of instances where PAYEMS increases by the specified trigger, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When GDP growth is strong but the federal funds rate remains exceptionally low, it signals that the central bank is intentionally keeping policy accommodative despite economic strength. This combination suggests future inflationary pressures and eventual policy tightening, driving the 10-year U.S. Treasury yield upwards over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth > 3% AND FEDFUNDS < 0.25%
regime_hint: post-recession recovery with accommodative monetary policy
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward yield direction is negative in more than 55% of periods where both GDPC1 and FEDFUNDS meet the specified conditions, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: In a low inflation environment, any unexpected rise in CPIAUCSL, even if modest, can trigger market expectations of future monetary tightening, especially if the economy is recovering. This shift in expectations drives the 10-year U.S. Treasury yield upwards over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth > 0.3% AND CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth < 2.5%
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward yield direction is negative in more than 50% of triggered instances within a low inflation recovery regime, this hypothesis is rejected.
